summary: This role is a poor fit because it requires high-level quantitative reasoning and complex physics, which directly conflicts with the candidate's struggles in math and analytical deduction. Furthermore, the extreme physical toll and 90% injury rate pose a severe threat to the candidate's priority of long-term personal wellbeing and sustainability. The prevalence of mandatory on-call rotations and high-stakes clinical liability makes achieving a predictable, low-stress work-life balance highly unlikely in this profession.
